metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC3's `grep` proof command to match the actual assertion syntax used in the test files: replace `swap is True` with `.get..swap.. is True` (or `"swap": True`) and replace `REB-2., .REB-1` with the key prefix actually used in the new tests (e.g. `DIG-` or document that `REB-` keys are required), so the command returns a hit when and only when the orientation guard is present.
evidence: Plan AC3: `grep -n "swap is True\|REB-2., .REB-1" tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**/test_link*.py`
test_link_dedup.py:128: `assert _emit("depends_on").get("swap") is True` — literal string `swap is True` is absent
Search for `REB-` in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_dedup.py and test_link_removal_sync.py both return zero matches
Both files use `DIG-` (test_link_dedup.py) and `PROJ-` (test_link_removal_sync.py) key prefixes, not `REB-`
findings.finding: AC3's `grep` proof command `grep -n "swap is True\|REB-2., .REB-1" tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**/test_link*.py` cannot validate its stated criterion: (a) the existing `swap` assertion in test_link_dedup.py:128 is written as `.get("swap") is True`, not the literal string `swap is True`, so the pattern fails to match the only currently-passing test that checks orientation; (b) both existing test files use `DIG-` and `PROJ-` Jira keys exclusively — the pattern `REB-2., .REB-1` matches nothing today and will only match if the new parametrized tests happen to be written with `REB-` keys, a constraint the plan never states. A passing proof command that returns zero hits is indistinguishable from a missing test.
scenarios: A developer writes the new parametrized depends_on test using `DIG-` keys and the correct `.get('swap') is True` assertion — the test correctly guards the c8ed fix but the AC3 proof command returns zero hits, giving a false 'not implemented' signal.
A reviewer runs the grep to confirm AC3 and gets no output, concluding the guard was not written, even though the underlying logic is correct.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a grep pattern that matches the realistic assertion form: `grep -rn 'swap.*True\|swap.*False\|depends_on' tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link*.py` — or, more precisely, specify in the plan that the new parametrized test must use the string `swap is True` literally in a comment or assertion message so the grep reliably fires.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update AC3's grep proof to use a key-name-agnostic pattern that actually matches the test file's `DIG-` key convention (e.g. `grep -n "swap.*True\|to_key.*DIG-1\|DIG-1.*to_key"`) or add a note that the parametrized test MUST use `REB-` keys if the REB-pattern grep is to be the proof.
evidence: Existing test_link_dedup.py uses StubBindingStore({'local-a': 'DIG-1', 'local-b': 'DIG-2'}) throughout — no REB-* keys appear anywhere in the file.
The test_outbound_diff_links_marks_swap_by_relation test (lines 115-129) already asserts swap=True for depends_on using DIG- keys, not REB- keys.

A `grep -n "REB-2., .REB-1" tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**/test_link*.py` on the current codebase returns zero matches, meaning any new test that follows the file's naming convention would also return zero matches and the second OR-branch of the proof never fires.
findings.finding: AC3's grep proof command uses the key pattern `REB-2., .REB-1` to verify the swapped `(to_key, issue_key)` set_relationship call, but every existing test in test_link_dedup.py uses `DIG-1`/`DIG-2` keys. If the new parametrized test follows the same convention (or uses any non-`REB-` key scheme), the grep returns zero matches, making the proof a silent false-negative that neither confirms the guard was written nor fails the gate.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3 — proof: `grep -n "swap is True\|REB-2., .REB-1" tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**/test_link*.py`
scenarios: Author writes the new parametrized test using DIG-1/DIG-2 (matching the file convention). The `swap is True` half of the grep matches, but the `REB-2., .REB-1` half never does — so if a reviewer checks the grep proof literally, it appears only half-verified or the key-direction assertion is unchecked.
Author writes the test using REB-1/REB-2 to satisfy the grep but the file now has inconsistent key naming with no functional benefit, creating noise in future reviews.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC3 proof grep pattern to use key prefixes consistent with the target test files (`DIG-` or `PROJ-`) so the oracle actually detects the `(to_key, issue_key)` orientation assertion — e.g. `grep -n "swap is True\|DIG-2.*DIG-1\|to_key.*DIG-1" tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**/test_link*.py`.
evidence: AC3 states: `grep -n "swap is True\|REB-2., .REB-1" tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**/test_link*.py`
test_link_dedup.py uses 'DIG-1'/'DIG-2' keys throughout (lines 86, 104, 125, 133, 140)
test_link_removal_sync.py uses 'PROJ-1'/'PROJ-2' keys throughout (lines 112, 128, 143, 159)
No 'REB-' prefix appears in either target file (search confirmed 0 matches)
findings.finding: The AC3 grep proof command uses Jira key prefix 'REB-' (`grep -n "swap is True\|REB-2., .REB-1"`) but both target test files use 'DIG-' (test_link_dedup.py) and 'PROJ-' (test_link_removal_sync.py) key prefixes. If the implementer follows existing naming conventions the grep will return 0 matches and pass vacuously, silently failing to verify the orientation assertion actually exists — the very guard the AC is meant to prove.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC3 proof command: `grep -n "swap is True\|REB-2., .REB-1" tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**/test_link*.py`
scenarios: Implementer writes the new parametrized test using 'DIG-' or 'PROJ-' keys (matching existing style) — grep yields 0 matches, CI step passes vacuously, the orientation guard is never verified.
Implementer uses 'REB-' keys to satisfy the grep — creates an inconsistency with the rest of the module's key naming convention.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `REB-2., .REB-1` in the grep with a pattern matching the actual key format the new tests will use, e.g. `DIG-2.*DIG-1` (if using the DIG- convention from test_link_dedup.py) or expand to cover both: `grep -n "swap is True\|DIG-2.*DIG-1\|PROJ-2.*PROJ-1" tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**/test_link*.py`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC3's grep oracle with the Scope: either add an applier-side test (scoped to one of the two test files) that asserts `set_relationship(REB-2, REB-1, ...)` for the `depends_on` case, or replace the `REB-2., .REB-1` half of the grep with a differ-level assertion that is actually produced by the scoped differ test (e.g. `swap.*True.*depends_on\|depends_on.*swap.*True`).
evidence: AC3 grep pattern: `grep -n "swap is True\|REB-2., .REB-1" tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**/test_link*.py`
Scope for test_link_dedup.py: 'parametrize outbound _diff_links emission over ALL relations — assert (jira_type, swap) for each MAPPED relation' — differ-side only
No applier-side test is mentioned anywhere in the Scope section
The `REB-2., .REB-1` pattern implies a `set_relationship(REB-2, REB-1)` assertion in an applier test, which does not correspond to any scoped file or task in the plan
findings.finding: AC3's grep proof command includes the pattern `REB-2., .REB-1` to assert the applier-side swapped `set_relationship(REB-2, REB-1, ...)` call, but the plan's Scope section never instructs any applier-side test to be written. The `test_link_dedup.py` scope covers the differ, not the applier, so the second half of the AC3 grep oracle references an artifact that the plan never specifies producing — making AC3 structurally unverifiable as written.
scenarios: An executor writes the differ test asserting `swap is True`, runs the AC3 grep, gets zero matches on `REB-2., .REB-1`, and has no plan instruction to resolve the discrepancy — leaving AC3 perpetually failing.
An executor interprets the grep as requiring an applier-side test and writes one ad-hoc, with no specification to guide correctness.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC3 grep's second alternative `REB-2., .REB-1` with a pattern that matches the differ-level assertion already scoped — e.g. `swap.*True` or `assert.*swap.*True` — since the plan only scopes differ tests. If applier coverage is also desired, add an explicit Scope bullet for a new test in `test_link_dedup.py` or `test_link_removal_sync.py` that stubs the applier call and asserts the swapped `(to_key, issue_key)` argument order.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `**` glob in AC#3's grep command with an explicit, shell-portable path so the command reliably locates the target files without requiring `globstar`.
evidence: AC#3 proving command: `grep -n "swap is True\|REB-2., .REB-1" tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**/test_link*.py`
VERIFY-COMMAND DEFECT TAXONOMY type 3 (wrong shell behavior): `**` without globstar does not recurse
Target files are at `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link*.py` — one level below the glob's effective reach
findings.finding: The grep proving command in AC#3 uses a `**` glob (`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**/test_link*.py`) which does NOT recursively expand in standard bash unless `shopt -s globstar` is set. The target files live under `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/`, one directory deeper. Without globstar, bash treats `**` as a single `*` and the glob will not descend into `diffing/`, causing grep to receive no matching files (or the literal unexpanded string), silently making the proving command unreliable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC#3 grep command: `grep -n "swap is True\|REB-2., .REB-1" tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**/test_link*.py`
scenarios: An agent runs AC#3 in a standard bash shell without `shopt -s globstar`; the glob fails to expand to the actual test files; grep either errors (no files) or silently misses the assertion strings; the AC cannot be verified as written.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the glob with an explicit path list:
```
grep -n "swap is True\|REB-2., .REB-1" \
  tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_dedup.py \
  tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_removal_sync.py
```
This is portable across bash/zsh without any shell option prerequisites.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'Why' section's coverage claim: `depends_on` has one spot-test for swap orientation in test_link_dedup.py (line 128), but `relates_to`, `duplicates`, `supersedes`, and `discovered_from` have zero unit tests — the true gap is the unmapped/skip-path coverage, not a complete absence of `depends_on`.
evidence: test_link_dedup.py:117-129: `test_outbound_diff_links_marks_swap_by_relation` explicitly emits a `depends_on` dep and asserts `swap is True`
Search for `depends_on` in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing returns this hit in test_link_dedup.py:117,128
Plan claim: 'used mechanical stub assertions, and the only semantic round-trip oracle … is gated behind live Jira' — partially correct but the spot-test of `swap` for `depends_on` already exists
findings.finding: The plan states 'the link-sync tests exercised the relation vocabulary with a single representative (blocks, ~19×; depends_on 0×)' — but the current test_link_dedup.py already contains `test_outbound_diff_links_marks_swap_by_relation` (line 115-129) which explicitly tests `depends_on` orientation (`_emit("depends_on").get("swap") is True`). Calling `depends_on 0×` overstates the gap, potentially causing a developer to skip verifying whether the new parametrized test genuinely adds coverage beyond the already-existing spot-test.
scenarios: A plan reader interprets 'depends_on 0×' as meaning no depends_on test exists anywhere in the unit suite, and therefore treats the AC3 swap-orientation guard as net-new — when in fact the existing test already covers swap orientation in a limited (non-parametrized) way.
A reviewer does not insist the new parametrized test provides meaningfully different coverage from the already-existing spot-check, since the diagnosis appeared correct.

findings.finding: The plan cites outbound_links.py:193-202 as the location of the REMOVE path's inward/outward direction logic, but the actual inward/outward branch (if inward_key: / elif outward_key: / relation = 'depends_on' if base_relation == 'blocks') runs from line 198 to line 205. Line 193 is a `continue` for unmapped link types, not the direction logic.
findings.location: Scope section: 'the untested branch of the REMOVE path's own inward/outward direction logic at outbound_links.py:193-202'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'the untested branch of the REMOVE path's own inward/outward direction logic at outbound_links.py:193-202' to 'outbound_links.py:198-205'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add one sentence of positive rationale in 'What' or 'Scope' stating why `_diff_links`-level parametrization is preferred (e.g. 'testing at `_diff_links` keeps fixtures minimal and isolates the vocabulary mapping from the managed-refs gating logic').
evidence: ABSENCE: neither 'What' nor 'Scope' explains why _diff_links-level unit parametrization is the right granularity.
'What' says only 'Close the sibling gaps by PARAMETRIZING the link tests over the FULL relation vocabulary' with no boundary justification.
findings.finding: The plan's 'What' and 'Scope' sections describe the mechanism (parametrize over the full vocabulary, add a REMOVE case) but give no positive rationale for WHY parametrization at the `_diff_links` unit level was chosen over alternatives (e.g. parametrizing at the `compute_outbound_mutations` integration level). Without a stated rationale the implementer has no guard against scope creep or choosing the wrong test boundary.
scenarios: Implementer opts to parametrize at the higher compute_outbound_mutations level, which would require a full Jira snapshot stub and managed_refs fixture for every relation — higher fixture burden and less isolated.

findings.finding: AC3 ('Reverting the c8ed production fix makes the parametrized depends_on case FAIL — verified locally, not committed') has no runnable proving command that produces durable, auditable evidence; it is an ephemeral manual step whose outcome cannot be reproduced or verified in a later session or by a reviewing agent.
findings.suggested_fix: Tag the AC line: `[operator-attested] Reverting the c8ed fix (re-drop the swap) makes the parametrized depends_on case FAIL — record the failing pytest output as a comment on this ticket.`

findings.finding: AC line 3 ('Reverting the c8ed production fix … verified locally, not committed') describes a verification exercise with no corresponding described work in the Scope section and no CI-verifiable proof, making it an orphan criterion that an implementer cannot demonstrate in a PR or automated run.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — item 3 ('Reverting the c8ed production fix …')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add a bullet such as: 'Locally verify that temporarily dropping the `swap=True` in outbound_links.py causes the parametrized `depends_on` case to FAIL (author attests in PR description; not committed).' Then update AC line 3 to say 'Author attests in PR description that this local check passed' so reviewers know the expected evidence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC3's 'applier side' set_relationship assertion with the Scope: either add an explicit Scope entry naming the applier-side test file/location, or clarify that AC3's applier-side clause is already covered by an existing (unmodified) test and explain which one.
evidence: AC3: 'the SWAPPED (to_key, issue_key) set_relationship call on the applier side'
Scope item 1: 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_dedup.py: parametrize outbound _diff_links emission' — differ only

Scope item 2: 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_removal_sync.py: add a REMOVE case … assert the emitted mutation is {action: remove, relation: depends_on}' — mutation shape only, not applier's set_relationship
No Scope item names a test_link_applier.py or equivalent file
findings.finding: AC3 requires asserting 'the SWAPPED (to_key, issue_key) set_relationship call on the applier side' but no Scope item names an applier-side test file or describes work to add that assertion; the only Scope files are a differ test (test_link_dedup.py) and a removal-mutation test (test_link_removal_sync.py). An implementer following Scope would have no clear deliverable covering the applier-side clause of AC3.
scenarios: An implementer writes only the differ-side swap assertion in test_link_dedup.py and the removal mutation assertion in test_link_removal_sync.py, satisfying Scope completely but leaving AC3's applier-side clause unsatisfied — causing unexpected CI failure or confusion about where to add the missing assertion.
AC3's grep pattern (grep -n "swap is True|REB-2., .REB-1") may pass if the differ-side assertion already contains the key strings, silently masking the missing applier-side coverage.
findings.suggested_fix: In Scope, add a third bullet: 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_applier.py (or equivalent): add/extend the depends_on case to assert the SWAPPED (to_key, issue_key) argument order in the set_relationship call, so a re-drop of the swap fix fails the test.' Alternatively, if an existing test already covers this, cite it explicitly in AC3's proof command.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
